target.description:
Target is the device ID this event is routed to. Set automatically from
the URL path parameter when submitted via /api/v1/devices/{device}/events.

BearerAuth.description: Required when server.addr is not a loopback address. Token value from the env var named by server.admin_token_env.
BearerAuth.name: Authorization

info.description: HTTP proxy for ESP8266 LED matrix controllers. Send events, rules pick animations, the scheduler owns the TCP connection.
info.title: LED Matrix Proxy

get.description: Returns only generated/frame animations that can be submitted to /play or /notify. Firmware preset IDs are excluded.
Returns all registered animations including firmware presets. Each entry exposes id, kind, and playable. Firmware presets have playable=false and may include effect_id, interval, and color.
Returns the sorted list of device IDs as defined in the config file.
Returns the current desired idle animation for this device. When restore_on_idle is true, the scheduler converges back to this animation whenever the play queue empties.

put.description: Replaces the desired idle animation without restarting the service. The scheduler converges to the new animation at the next idle opportunity. Set restore_on_idle=false or omit animation to disable idle convergence.

400.description: Unknown animation ID or validation error

put.summary: Set idle background animation at runtime

post.description: Publishes a normalized event for async rule processing. Known override attributes (animation, restore, duration, interrupt_mode) are validated synchronously before publishing.
Sets global brightness (0–255). Changes take effect immediately.
Sets all pixels to black. If restore_on_idle is enabled, the idle background will be reapplied afterward.
Sets every pixel to a single RGB color immediately.
post.summary: Fill display with a solid colour

post.description: Starts a built-in firmware animation effect with custom interval and color. Use effect_id=0 to stop the current effect.
Publishes an event with source=http and type=notify. Rules matching that source/type decide which animation plays.
Directly enqueues a generated/frame animation on the device scheduler. Does not accept firmware preset IDs; use /preset/{animation} for those.

400.description: Unknown, non-playable animation, or invalid parameters

post.description: Looks up a registered firmware_preset animation and sends its effect_id, interval, and color to the device. The idle background is restored afterward if restore_on_idle is enabled. Use GET /api/v1/animations/catalog to discover preset IDs.

400.description: Animation exists but is not a firmware preset

post.summary: Play a firmware preset by animation ID

delete.description: Removes all pending items from the queue. Returns the number of cleared items.

get.description: Returns current queue depth, scheduler state, and snapshot of queued items.
